message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -191,7 +191,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1ifi","title":"Fix off-by-one in lease_conflict.rs and control_channel.rs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-27T06:03:35.943712705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T06:18:24.323041184Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T06:18:24.322988526Z","close_reason":"Fixed off-by-one in lease_conflict.rs (expires_at >= now -> > now for fail-closed expiry) and control_channel.rs (replay window trim > -> >= for exact size enforcement). 38 targeted tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1ikg","title":"fix(tools): NaN/Inf fail-open in containment_revocation_metrics latency validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-07T11:01:14.342049458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T11:01:26.526871034Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T11:01:26.526751621Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1irxh","title":"Verifier SDK replay capsule validation drift and underbound binding hash","description":"Random deep audit found that sdk::verifier_sdk::verify_capsule has drifted from the canonical replay_capsule validator and computes binding_hash from only capsule_id, format_version, and inputs_len. That allows distinct capsules with the same ID/version/input count to share a binding hash, weakening report identity and chain uniqueness. Fix by reusing canonical replay-capsule validation/replay semantics and binding to full capsule contents.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FoggySnow","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:53:03.007562511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T02:59:30.480880896Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T02:59:30.480860628Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixes landed; local structural checks and pytest suites passed; rch cargo validation blocked by unrelated franken_engine compile errors outside touched files","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1itr9","title":"Shared blocker: rch-offloaded franken-node validation stalls or SIGKILLs before target tests execute","description":"Why this exists:\nMultiple active franken_node beads are now source-complete or near source-complete but cannot be closed because rch-offloaded focused validation either stalls in remote_exec_start after reaching frankenengine-engine, or dies earlier with worker/resource failure while compiling the sibling path dependency. This is now a project-level blocker, not an isolated bead issue.\n\nObserved evidence in current coordination window:\n- bd-3rkin / LavenderRobin: build 29750734287276350 on vmi1264463 reached Compiling frankenengine-engine, then progress_stale=true in remote_exec_start and was cancelled cleanly.\n- bd-2r9uf / BoldLake: focused run on vmi1167313 exited 101 because frankenengine-engine compile was SIGKILLed before remote_bulkhead tests ran.\n- multiple prior beads record the same remote_exec_start stall or upstream frankenengine-engine compile failure pattern.\n\nWhat this bug must do:\n1. Determine whether the dominant blocker is rch orchestration, worker resource pressure, frankenengine-engine compile pressure, or a mix of them.\n2. Produce at least one reliable, repeatable validation path for focused frankenengine-node cargo tests via rch, or identify the exact upstream source fix/config change required.\n3. Record the recommended operator workflow so future beads stop rediscovering the same failure mode.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. A fresh investigator can reproduce the blocker from the bead notes without tribal knowledge.\n2. The investigation identifies a decisive root-cause class with concrete evidence (not just "stalled").\n3. Either a reliable rch validation recipe exists, or the exact upstream code/config fix surface is named and linked to evidence.\n4. At least one currently blocked franken_node bead can reference this blocker instead of carrying duplicate bespoke explanations.","notes":"CopperAnchor support slice 2026-03-23:
- Dry-run comparison is inconsistent: env-style `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1` selected saturated `vmi1149989` (`slots_available=0`), while explicit `cargo check -j 1` selected `vmi1227854` (`slots_available=4`) but still reported `estimated_cores=4`.
- Local rch config at investigation time: `build_slots=4`, `test_slots=4`, `check_slots=2`, `min_free_gb=80.0`. Dry-run still selected `vmi1227854` even though status showed only ~56.3 GB free, so `min_free_gb` is not behaving like a hard exclusion.
- Live probe `rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-copperanchor-bd-1itr9-j1 cargo check -j 1 -p frankenengine-node --lib` selected `jain` anyway, completed as build `29750734287276398`, and exited `101` quickly instead of stalling.
- Deterministic upstream failures surfaced: missing `include_str!` JSON assets in `claim_entitlement.rs`, `claim_envelope_contract.rs`, `module_compatibility_matrix.rs`, `rgc_planning_track.rs`, and `zero_placeholder_scan.rs`, plus real `E0308` type mismatches in `native_addon_parity_gate.rs:774,786,798,810`.
- Critical local-vs-remote inference: those JSON files exist locally under `/data/projects/franken_engine/docs/`, but the rch sync log only transferred `crates/franken-engine` and `crates/franken-extension-host`, not the sibling `docs/` tree. This points to an rch dependency-sync boundary bug for path dependencies that use `include_str!("../../../docs/...json")`, plus separate real franken_engine compile errors after sync succeeds.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"LavenderRobin","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:47:34.066465155Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:28:42.098983478Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":286,"issue_id":"bd-1itr9","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"Initial investigation baseline captured. Current rch state on this machine: rch doctor --verbose passes with one cancellation-health warning; rch workers probe --all reports 8/8 workers reachable; rch status shows remote-ready posture but multiple open circuits plus critical/elevated storage pressure on several workers. The first working hypothesis is mixed failure modes: worker pressure/resource exhaustion on some lanes, plus remote_exec_start stale-state on others after entering frankenengine-engine compilation. Next step is to gather a concise operator recipe and determine whether there is any worker/flag combination that yields a stable focused frankenengine-node test run.","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:48:02Z"},{"id":287,"issue_id":"bd-1itr9","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"Follow-up evidence: rch workers capabilities --refresh confirms the worker fleet is not failing because of obvious runtime skew. All probed workers report Rust 1.95.0-nightly and Bun 1.3.9, with only an npm major-version mismatch versus local. Combined with rch workers probe --all (8/8 reachable), this narrows the blocker away from basic worker availability/toolchain absence and toward a mixed class of failures: (a) worker pressure / rustc SIGKILL during frankenengine-engine compilation on some lanes, (b) deterministic upstream sibling compile errors on others, and (c) remote_exec_start stale-state after entering frankenengine-engine on others. New datapoint from ScarletOtter on bd-1896m: build 29750734287276356 on vmi1293453 failed in /data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/frankenlab_release_gate_promotion.rs with E0609 (no field kind on GatePromotionEntry), which is a decisive upstream compile blocker rather than a franken_node test failure.","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:49:10Z"},{"id":288,"issue_id":"bd-1itr9","author":"BoldLake","text":"2026-03-23 BoldLake blocker analysis from the franken_node side: current rch failure mode is decisively scheduler/resource-side, not a newly discovered franken_node source regression. Evidence: (1) `rch status --workers --jobs -j` now reports only 13 successes vs 87 failures across the last 100 remote builds. (2) Several workers are preflight-bad on pressure alone: vmi1156319 has ~2 GB free, vmi1149989 ~18 GB, vmi1153651 ~32 GB, while vmi1264463 and vmi1293453 are running at ~90 memory-pressure. (3) The only clearly healthy idle box is jain, but ~/.config/rch/config.toml currently requires build_slots=4, test_slots=4, check_slots=2 while ~/.config/rch/workers.toml gives jain total_slots=1, so jain cannot serve our cargo check/test lanes under current local config. (4) Healthy-capacity workers are already occupied by long-lived remote_exec_start builds from other projects: at snapshot time vmi1227854 and vmi1293453 were carrying stale franken_engine / pi_agent_rust jobs, and vmi1264463 also had a mixed stale/active queue. (5) `rch diagnose --dry-run -j -- cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib` and the focused franken_node test probes both claimed success by selecting vmi1167313, but reported slots_available=0, which points to a scheduler/preflight inconsistency or stale-slot accounting bug on top of the raw worker pressure problem. Recommended operator workflow until the fleet is remediated: run `rch status --workers --jobs -j` first, do not start new focused franken_node validation if the only idle healthy worker is jain while slot requirements remain >1, and require `rch diagnose --dry-run -j -- <command>` to return a non-null worker that is not effectively saturated before spending a build slot. Concrete fix surfaces are outside this repo: local rch slot-selection config plus fleet pressure cleanup / scheduler selection behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:52:33Z"},{"id":289,"issue_id":"bd-1itr9","author":"AmberHeron","text":"AmberHeron investigation slice:

New evidence points to a mixed blocker class with a scheduler/admission inconsistency on top of the existing worker-pressure problem.

1. Local rch config on this machine is force_remote=true with build_slots=4, test_slots=4, check_slots=2, selection.min_free_gb=80.0. `jain` is the healthiest idle worker in status output but advertises total_slots=1, so the default slot policy does not obviously make it the preferred target for normal build/test lanes.

2. `rch diagnose --dry-run -j` is internally inconsistent for cargo lanes right now. On the same snapshot it reported:
- `cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib` -> worker `vmi1153651`, `estimated_cores=4`, `slots_available=2`
- `cargo build -p frankenengine-node --lib` -> same worker `vmi1153651`, same `estimated_cores=4`, same `slots_available=2`
- `cargo test -p frankenengine-node --lib release_removes_matching_convergence_when_zone_has_multiple_quarantines -- --exact --nocapture` -> same worker `vmi1153651`, same `estimated_cores=4`, same `slots_available=2`
- even `env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-amberheron-bd-1itr9-check cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib` dry-run bounced to a *different* worker (`vmi1227854`) instead of surfacing a stable low-slot path

3. Live reproduction through rch using a unique target dir and low-contention flags:
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/target_rch_amberheron_bd1itr9_release CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTFLAGS=-Cdebuginfo=0 rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenengine-node --lib release_removes_matching_convergence_when_zone_has_multiple_quarantines -- --exact --nocapture`

Observed result:
- initial rch log selected `vmi1149989` and printed `Selected worker: vmi1149989 ... (0 slots, speed 50.0)`
- sync completed cleanly for the franken_node + sibling dependency roots
- remote output progressed through dependency compilation and reached `Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0 (/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine)`
- after that it went output-silent
- status snapshot showed build `29750734287276389` on `vmi1149989` with `heartbeat_detail=remote_exec_start`, `progress_age_secs=135`, `detector_progress_stale=true`, `detector_slots_owned=2`
- I cancelled it cleanly with `rch cancel 29750734287276389 --json`, which returned `status=cancelled` and `slots_released=2`

4. During the same investigation window, `rch status --workers --jobs -j` briefly showed another franken_node build on `jain` using a low-slot cargo-check style command (`env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-darkriver-bd-1itr9-check cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib`). That suggests a constrained cargo-check path *may* sometimes fit on `jain`, but the current dry-run selection path is not surfacing it deterministically.

Conclusion: the blocker is not just "frankenengine-engine compile is heavy". There is also a real scheduler/admission mismatch: dry-run and live selection disagree, live selection can choose a worker while logging `0 slots`, and focused tests still reproduce the post-compile `remote_exec_start` stale state even with isolated target dirs and `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1`. I do not have a reliable focused `cargo test` recipe yet; the exact fix surface still points outside this repo to rch worker selection / slot accounting plus fleet pressure management.","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:20:23Z"}]}

Concrete fix surfaces are outside this repo: local rch slot-selection config plus fleet pressure cleanup / scheduler selection behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-23T07:52:33Z"},{"id":289,"issue_id":"bd-1itr9","author":"AmberHeron","text":"AmberHeron investigation slice:

New evidence points to a mixed blocker class with a scheduler/admission inconsistency on top of the existing worker-pressure problem.

1. Local rch config on this machine is force_remote=true with build_slots=4, test_slots=4, check_slots=2, selection.min_free_gb=80.0. `jain` is the healthiest idle worker in status output but advertises total_slots=1, so the default slot policy does not obviously make it the preferred target for normal build/test lanes.

2. `rch diagnose --dry-run -j` is internally inconsistent for cargo lanes right now. On the same snapshot it reported:
- `cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib` -> worker `vmi1153651`, `estimated_cores=4`, `slots_available=2`
- `cargo build -p frankenengine-node --lib` -> same worker `vmi1153651`, same `estimated_cores=4`, same `slots_available=2`
- `cargo test -p frankenengine-node --lib release_removes_matching_convergence_when_zone_has_multiple_quarantines -- --exact --nocapture` -> same worker `vmi1153651`, same `estimated_cores=4`, same `slots_available=2`
- even `env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-amberheron-bd-1itr9-check cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib` dry-run bounced to a *different* worker (`vmi1227854`) instead of surfacing a stable low-slot path

3. Live reproduction through rch using a unique target dir and low-contention flags:
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/projects/franken_node/target_rch_amberheron_bd1itr9_release CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUSTFLAGS=-Cdebuginfo=0 rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenengine-node --lib release_removes_matching_convergence_when_zone_has_multiple_quarantines -- --exact --nocapture`

Observed result:
- initial rch log selected `vmi1149989` and printed `Selected worker: vmi1149989 ... (0 slots, speed 50.0)`
- sync completed cleanly for the franken_node + sibling dependency roots
- remote output progressed through dependency compilation and reached `Compiling frankenengine-engine v0.1.0 (/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine)`
- after that it went output-silent
- status snapshot showed build `29750734287276389` on `vmi1149989` with `heartbeat_detail=remote_exec_start`, `progress_age_secs=135`, `detector_progress_stale=true`, `detector_slots_owned=2`
- I cancelled it cleanly with `rch cancel 29750734287276389 --json`, which returned `status=cancelled` and `slots_released=2`

4. During the same investigation window, `rch status --workers --jobs -j` briefly showed another franken_node build on `jain` using a low-slot cargo-check style command (`env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target-darkriver-bd-1itr9-check cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib`). That suggests a constrained cargo-check path *may* sometimes fit on `jain`, but the current dry-run selection path is not surfacing it deterministically.

Conclusion: the blocker is not just "frankenengine-engine compile is heavy". There is also a real scheduler/admission mismatch: dry-run and live selection disagree, live selection can choose a worker while logging `0 slots`, and focused tests still reproduce the post-compile `remote_exec_start` stale state even with isolated target dirs and `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1`. I do not have a reliable focused `cargo test` recipe yet; the exact fix surface still points outside this repo to rch worker selection / slot accounting plus fleet pressure management.","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:20:23Z"},{"id":290,"issue_id":"bd-1itr9","author":"NavyBeaver","text":"Taking over the shared rch blocker investigation from stale state. Immediate plan: (1) verify current rch fleet health and queue state, (2) compare worker-selection signals against config thresholds, (3) run one fresh rch-offloaded focused cargo reproduction with isolated target dir, and (4) record whether the dominant blocker is worker health, compile pressure, or path-dependency sync boundaries. I will route around source-complete beads and feed back a reusable operator recipe or a precise upstream fix surface.","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:07:33Z"},{"id":291,"issue_id":"bd-1itr9","author":"BrownOwl","text":"BrownOwl support slice 2026-03-23:
- Fresh local snapshot no longer supports a pure 'no-capacity' theory. `rch status --workers --jobs -j` currently shows posture=`degraded` but 7/8 workers healthy and 43/47 slots available. The only degraded worker is `jain` (telemetry gap / stale metrics), not the whole fleet.
- The stronger scheduler finding is that low-parallelism intent is not affecting slot estimation in dry-run selection. All of the following still estimate `4` cores in `rch diagnose --dry-run -j`:
  - `cargo check -j 1 -p frankenengine-node --lib`
  - `cargo test -j 1 -p frankenengine-node --lib release_removes_matching_convergence_when_zone_has_multiple_quarantines -- --exact --nocapture`
  - env-style forms with `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1` and isolated `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`
- This means both explicit `-j 1` and env-style `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1` are effectively ignored by the estimator for cargo check/test lanes right now. That is a plausible root cause for why constrained franken_node closeout probes do not route onto genuinely small-capacity lanes even when operators are trying to de-amplify them.
- Current dry-run examples from this machine:
  - `cargo check -j 1 ...` -> worker `vmi1149989`, `estimated_cores=4`, `slots_available=0` after selection
  - `cargo test -j 1 ...` -> worker `vmi1153651`, `estimated_cores=4`, `slots_available=4`
  - `env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 ... cargo test ...` -> same worker `vmi1153651`, still `estimated_cores=4`, `slots_available=0` after selection
- I also checked the sync-boundary hypothesis locally. `/data/projects/franken_engine/.rchignore` does **not** exclude `docs/`, while `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine` contains many `include_str!("../../../docs/...json")` callsites. That does not disprove the missing-doc transfer bug, but it does narrow it: the missing `docs/` payloads are more likely an rch path-dependency sync-root issue than an ignore-pattern issue in either repo.
- Net inference: the current blocker class still looks mixed, but there is now a more concrete scheduler/config surface to point at: cargo slot estimation is not respecting low-job intent, and docs-sync loss appears to happen above `.rchignore` evaluation.","created_at":"2026-03-23T20:09:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1iua","title":"fix(supply_chain): audit chain integrity after eviction + unbounded recall_receipts in quarantine","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-07T11:06:33.346817899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T11:06:46.308915497Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T11:06:46.308789152Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1iyx","title":"[10.14] Add determinism conformance tests ensuring identical artifacts across replicas for identical content/config.","description":"## Why This Exists
Determinism is a claim that must be proven, not assumed. Even with deterministic seed derivation (bd-29r6) in place, subtle sources of non-determinism (hash map iteration order, floating-point rounding, timestamp injection, thread scheduling) can cause replicas to diverge. This bead creates the conformance test infrastructure that empirically validates determinism by running multiple simulated replicas with identical inputs and comparing their outputs byte-for-byte. This is the verification layer that turns the determinism invariant from a design aspiration into a CI-enforced guarantee, directly serving the 9J enhancement map's requirement that franken_node's three-kernel architecture produces reproducible artifacts.

## What This Must Do
1. Create `tests/conformance/replica_artifact_determinism.rs` implementing a multi-replica test harness.
2. The harness instantiates N (configurable, default 3) independent processing pipelines with identical content and configuration, runs them to completion, and compares all output artifacts byte-for-byte.
3. If any divergence is detected, the test MUST report: (a) the first mismatched byte offset, (b) the artifact path/name that diverged, (c) a root-cause hint (if determinable, e.g., "timestamp field differs", "hash map ordering differs").
4. Create `fixtures/determinism/` directory containing at least 3 fixture sets (small, medium, edge-case) that exercise different encoding paths.
5. Generate `artifacts/10.14/determinism_conformance_results.csv` with columns: `fixture_name`, `replica_count`, `artifact_count`, `all_identical`, `first_divergence_artifact`, `first_divergence_offset`, `root_cause`.
6. Tests MUST run in CI as part of the standard test suite (not gated behind a feature flag).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Multi-replica fixture run yields byte-identical artifact sets; divergence test reports first mismatch and root cause; tests run in CI.
- ADDITIONAL: At least 3 fixture sets covering different encoding configurations.
- ADDITIONAL: Divergence reporting includes hex dump of mismatched region (16 bytes context).
- ADDITIONAL: Tests complete within 30 seconds for the standard fixture set.
- ADDITIONAL: Results CSV is machine-parseable and consumed by the section gate.

## Testing & Logging Requirements
- Unit tests: Comparison logic correctly identifies identical artifact sets; comparison logic correctly identifies and localizes divergence; root-cause hinting for common divergence patterns.
- Integration tests: Full multi-replica run with known-identical inputs produces PASS; intentionally injected non-determinism (e.g., timestamp in output) produces FAIL with correct diagnostics.
- Conformance tests: All published fixtures pass on all supported platforms.
- Adversarial tests: Inject subtle non-determinism sources (thread-dependent ordering, time-of-day) and confirm detection.
- Structured logs: Event codes `DETERMINISM_CHECK_STARTED`, `DETERMINISM_CHECK_PASSED`, `DETERMINISM_CHECK_FAILED` with fields: `fixture_name`, `replica_count`, `divergence_artifact`, `divergence_offset`, `trace_id`.

## Expected Artifacts
- `tests/conformance/replica_artifact_determinism.rs` -- multi-replica conformance test harness
- `fixtures/determinism/*` -- fixture input sets (at least 3)
- `artifacts/10.14/determinism_conformance_results.csv` -- results matrix
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-1iyx/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-1iyx/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies
- Upstream:
  - bd-29r6 (Implement content-derived deterministic seed derivation for encoding/repair schedules)
- Downstream (depends on this):
  - bd-3epz (Section-wide verification gate)
  - bd-5rh (Section 10.14 parent tracking bead)","acceptance_criteria":"Multi-replica fixture run yields byte-identical artifact sets; divergence test reports first mismatch and root cause; tests run in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:56.970468633Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T19:05:16.958115020Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T19:05:16.958087558Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iyx","depends_on_id":"bd-29r6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:15.147930610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
